rule: raw:m5.i4-schema-migration-retroactive-deepening-omega-cycle (parent: design/meta_engine/2026-04-26_m5_ordinal_infra_omega_cycle.json i4 axis, 18 LoC). Subject narrowed to the schema-mutation step itself: 4 new fields (ordinal_label, ordinal_predecessor, is_strict_descent, escape_basis) added to state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl rows.
subject: schema migration of state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl: 23 rows R4..R41, +4 fields per row (ordinal_label, ordinal_predecessor, is_strict_descent, escape_basis), schema_version 1.0 → 2.0. The mutation is the smallest unit of risk: data corruption, schema drift, downstream tool breakage if any reader assumes 1.0-shape. THIS witness deepens the i4 axis from 18 LoC tier-1 plan to a saturated risk-and-control ω-cycle.
purpose: Before mutating state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl, enumerate (Track-A) the controls that the migration MUST satisfy to be safe (raw 77 .bak audit-append-only, schema_version bump for forward-compat detection, downstream-reader inventory, forward-compat reader stub, reversible down-migration script, atomicity contract), and (Track-B) the limits that bound any migration (open-set reader discovery, git-lock contention during mutation, JSONL append vs in-place tension, per-reader strictness drift, schema-versioning is convention not theorem, no schema-validator gatekeeper exists). Goal: refuse to ship the mutation until each Tier-1 control is bound to a falsifier and the open-set Track-B limits are explicitly acknowledged.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'm5.i4 schema migration 에 대해 ω-cycle 발사 — code 는 쓰지 말고 witness 만'.
method: two-track multi-axis ideation, scoped to the i4 schema-mutation surface only. Track-A (controls, ≥6) = the operational guards that the mutation must wear (.bak, schema_version, reader inventory, forward-compat reader, down-migration, atomicity). Track-B (limits, ≥5) = the boundaries that cannot be eliminated (reader discovery is open-set, git-lock contention, append vs in-place, parser strictness varies, schema-versioning convention not theorem). dedup → cluster → Tier. omega_stop = enumeration fixpoint + each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate, theory_anchor, falsifier).
ordinal_self_stamp_basis: ATR_0 + 1 successor step. Inherited from parent ω-cycle's Γ_0 (m5 framework anchor) plus one ordinal step for the deepening: this witness is one stratum below the parent (i4 axis is the implementation-mutation; THIS is the risk-and-control plane of that mutation). Self-stratification per m5 dossier §Appendix and parent.raw_strategies_self_applied.self_reference_acknowledgment.
current_state_pre_migration: state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl — 23 rows R4..R41, schema_version absent (implicit 1.0), `round:int` only, no ordinal_label / predecessor / strict_descent / escape_basis
no forward-compat reader stub in tool/
no down-migration script — migration would currently be one-way

References found ONLY in: design/meta_engine/{2026-04-26_m3_cert_populator_omega_cycle.json, 2026-04-26_m5_ordinal_infra_omega_cycle.json, 2026-04-25_final_form_and_limits_omega_cycle.json, m3_closed_loop_cert_dossier.md, m5_ordinal_anchor_dossier.md, m6_falsifier_F1_dossier.md, m9_atomic_rollback_dossier.md}; design/cross_engine/r10_m10_coupling_dossier.md; design/roadmap_engine/2026-04-25_final_form_and_limits_omega_cycle.json; docs/atlas_meta_dashboard.md; state/meta_trigger_watchlist.jsonl; state/meta_axis_dependency.jsonl. ALL references are documentation/state-cross-link, NOT executable readers. No Python/shell parser currently consumes the file.

implication: at THIS moment the reader-set is effectively empty (zero executable consumers), but this is a SNAPSHOT. The first reader (m3 cert populator, m5 ordinal-infra parser+comparator, m9 atomic-rollback, fixpoint detector i5) will be authored AFTER the migration, against the 2.0 schema. Race window: any tool authored between schema bump and reader-completion that assumes 1.0 will break.
git status pre-migration: M state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl already absent (clean as of last commit 5891fab1); migration will produce a new modification.
known_blockers_inherited: raw 77 audit-append-only — mandates .bak before destructive overwrite (parent.tier_1[i4] already references this)
i14 retroactive-labeling integrity — pass-2 of i6 must be axes_acted-event-bound; this witness only handles the SCHEMA mutation, not the back-stamp values themselves

axes_surfaced.name: bak_before_mutation_raw_77
axes_surfaced.summary: BEFORE any write to state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl, create state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl.bak as a verbatim byte-copy of the pre-migration file. .bak written via tool/atomic_copy.sh equivalent (cp -p with permission preservation) followed by sha256sum recorded into state/meta_migration_audit.jsonl with {ts, sha256_pre, row_count_pre:23, schema_version_pre:'1.0_implicit'}. NO migration step proceeds until .bak sha256 is verified to match the source. raw 77 audit-append-only doctrine, hard prerequisite. 4 LoC.

axes_surfaced.name: schema_version_field_bump
axes_surfaced.summary: Add `schema_version: '2.0'` as the FIRST field of every row post-migration (and record schema_version_pre:'1.0_implicit' in audit). Forward-compat readers MUST switch on this field; readers that do not understand 2.0 must refuse rather than silently mis-parse. The bump itself is the trip-wire: any downstream tool that opens the file post-migration sees schema_version:'2.0' and must either (a) parse 2.0 fields or (b) skip with clear error. 2 LoC mutation per row.

axes_surfaced.name: downstream_reader_inventory
axes_surfaced.summary: BEFORE migration, run `grep -rl 'meta_engine_evolution_log' /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool /Users/ghost/core/nexus/scripts /Users/ghost/core/nexus/state /Users/ghost/core/nexus/design /Users/ghost/core/nexus/docs` and snapshot the result into state/meta_migration_audit.jsonl alongside the pre-sha256. Current grep result (this trawl): zero executable readers in tool/ or scripts/. References live in 7 design/* docs, 1 docs/, 2 state/* cross-link jsonls. Implication: migration is currently SAFE from breaking any executable reader. The inventory snapshot is the falsifier baseline — if a future ω-cycle finds a reader that did not appear in this snapshot, the migration's atomicity claim was scoped against an incomplete set (Track-B i6 acknowledges this). 6 LoC inventory + audit emit.

axes_surfaced.name: forward_compat_reader_skip_unknown
axes_surfaced.summary: Author tool/meta_engine_log_reader.py (or equivalent) BEFORE the migration that (a) reads schema_version, (b) for 2.0 returns full {round, axes_acted, ordinal_label, ordinal_predecessor, is_strict_descent, escape_basis, ...}, (c) for 1.0_implicit (no schema_version field) returns the legacy shape unchanged AND attaches a deprecation warning, (d) for any 3.0+ unknown version, gracefully skips unknown fields with logger.warning rather than KeyError. This reader is the canonical accessor — all future meta-engine consumers (m3 cert, m9 rollback, fixpoint detector i5, budget tracker i7) MUST use it. Forward-compat is the INVARIANT: unknown fields never raise. 15 LoC.

axes_surfaced.name: reversible_down_migration_script
axes_surfaced.summary: Author tool/meta_engine_log_down_migrate.py that takes a 2.0 file and produces a 1.0_implicit file by stripping the 4 new fields + schema_version. Down-migration is the FORMAL reversibility guarantee — .bak alone is the trivial rollback (file replacement), but down-migrate proves the schema delta is information-additive (the 4 new fields are projections of richer state, not lossy transforms of existing fields). Falsifier: down_migrate(up_migrate(file_v1)) must equal file_v1 byte-for-byte (sha256 match). 12 LoC. Pair with up-migration script for round-trip test.

axes_surfaced.name: atomicity_full_success_or_full_rollback
axes_surfaced.summary: Migration script writes to state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl.tmp (NOT in-place), validates row_count_post == 23 AND schema_version field present in every row AND all 4 new fields present per row, computes sha256_post, then atomic mv .tmp → final via os.rename (POSIX atomic on same filesystem). On ANY validation failure, .tmp is unlinked and original file is untouched (still equals .bak). On success, audit row {ts, sha256_pre, sha256_post, row_count:23, schema_version:'1.0_implicit→2.0', migrator_version:'i4-v1'} appended to state/meta_migration_audit.jsonl. Atomicity is the ALL-OR-NOTHING contract: post-script invocation, the file is either fully 1.0_implicit (failure) or fully 2.0 (success) — never partial. 10 LoC.

axes_surfaced.name: reader_discovery_is_open_set
axes_surfaced.summary: `grep -rl 'meta_engine_evolution_log'` returns only files that mention the literal path. Readers that compute the path (path = state_dir + '/' + name) or that consume the file via a generic state-snapshot loader will NOT show up in the grep. Therefore the i3 inventory is necessarily a LOWER BOUND on the actual reader set. Mitigation is structural not exhaustive: i4 forward-compat reader becomes the CANONICAL accessor and any future reader that bypasses it is a separate audit failure (caught by code-review or by m9 rollback log). HARD WALL: cannot prove reader set is empty; can only prove the discovered set is handled.

axes_surfaced.name: git_lock_during_migration
axes_surfaced.summary: If the migration runs while a git operation holds .git/index.lock (commit, rebase, checkout, merge), os.rename of the file may succeed but git's view of the working tree could race against an in-flight git add. Practical impact: low (single-developer repo, migration is a one-shot manual run), but theoretical impact: the migration's atomicity (i6) is filesystem-atomic, NOT git-atomic. Mitigation: migrator script checks for .git/index.lock existence and refuses to run if present (logger.error 'git operation in flight — abort'). 2 LoC guard. Documents the fact that filesystem atomicity ≠ VCS atomicity.

axes_surfaced.name: jsonl_append_vs_in_place_tension
axes_surfaced.summary: JSONL is canonically append-only — adding new ROWS is safe (no existing reader breaks). MUTATING existing rows (this migration) violates the append-only convention. The migration is therefore a POINT-IN-TIME exception requiring explicit out-of-band justification (parent.tier_1[i4] rationale). Tension: in-place mutation breaks the JSONL invariant; pure append (e.g., emit new rows R4'..R41' alongside originals) doubles row count and breaks ROUND-uniqueness. Choice (per parent i4): mutate in place once, with .bak + schema_version + atomicity as the controls. Track-B documents that this choice has cost: any future tool that assumed 'rows are immutable once written' must be reviewed.

axes_surfaced.name: downstream_parser_strictness_varies
axes_surfaced.summary: Different parsers handle unknown fields differently: stdlib json.loads accepts and surfaces unknown keys (caller decides); pydantic strict mode raises ValidationError on unknown fields; jq with explicit field selectors silently ignores; bash awk-style parsers may fail on field-position assumptions. The i4 forward-compat reader sets a CANONICAL contract (graceful unknown-field skip), but cannot enforce it on third-party or ad-hoc parsers. Mitigation: i3 inventory + i4 canonical accessor + reviewer hygiene. The strictness-variance is a permanent ecosystem fact — no migration can normalize it.

axes_surfaced.name: schema_versioning_convention_not_theorem
axes_surfaced.summary: schema_version:'2.0' is a string field. Nothing in the JSON spec, the JSONL convention, or the filesystem enforces that readers READ this field, RESPECT this field, or BUMP it correctly on future migrations. There is no schema-validator gatekeeper (e.g., no JSON Schema check on commit, no pre-commit hook validating shape). The version field is a SOCIAL CONTRACT — it works only if every reader honors it. Mitigation: i4 canonical accessor mechanizes the social contract for the in-house consumer set; falsifier F-m5-i4-VerDrift fires if any reader is found in a future audit that ignores schema_version. HARD WALL: cannot upgrade convention to theorem without a CI/pre-commit validator (separate axis, deferred).

dedupe_method: (1) i1 .bak vs i6 atomicity: i1 is the rollback substrate (file copy), i6 is the all-or-nothing contract (validation + atomic rename) — complementary, both keep. (2) i2 schema_version vs i11 versioning-convention: i2 is the mechanism (bump field), i11 is the limit (no enforcement) — i11 binds i2's effectiveness. (3) i3 reader inventory vs i7 open-set: i3 is the snapshot, i7 acknowledges incompleteness — i7 binds i3. (4) i4 forward-compat reader vs i10 parser-strictness-varies: i4 sets canonical contract, i10 acknowledges third-party drift — i10 binds i4's reach. (5) i5 down-migration vs i9 append-vs-in-place: i5 proves reversibility, i9 frames why reversibility matters (in-place mutation is the JSONL exception) — i9 motivates i5. (6) i6 atomicity vs i8 git-lock: i6 is filesystem-atomic, i8 acknowledges VCS-atomic is different — i8 binds i6. cluster: B=backup-and-rollback (i1/i5/i6), V=version-and-discovery (i2/i3/i7/i11), R=reader-contract (i4/i10), I=invariant-tension (i8/i9). NO axis rejected — all 11 are load-bearing.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: hard prerequisite per raw 77. Without .bak, NO migration step is permitted. Trivial rollback substrate.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: raw 77 audit-append-only; sha256-witnessed copy is the rollback ground truth
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: sha256(.bak) MUST equal sha256(state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl) at moment-of-copy. ANY mismatch = F-m5-i4-BakDrift fires, abort migration before mutation begins.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: all-or-nothing contract. Filesystem-level atomic via os.rename; validation gate ensures partial-write states never reach the final path. Without this, .bak rollback is reactive; with this, failure mode is non-existent at the file path.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: POSIX rename(2) atomicity on same filesystem; tmp+validate+swap pattern
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: post-script invocation, sha256(state/meta_engine_evolution_log.jsonl) MUST equal EITHER sha256_pre (failure path) OR sha256_post (success path). NEVER any third hash. ANY third hash = F-m5-i4-AtomBreak fires.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: the trip-wire that enables i4 forward-compat reader to dispatch correctly. Without schema_version:'2.0' on every row, no reader can distinguish migrated from legacy.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: semantic versioning convention applied at row level (not file level — JSONL is a row stream)
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: every row in post-migration file MUST contain `schema_version:'2.0'` as a key. Any row missing schema_version = F-m5-i4-VerMissing fires.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: canonical accessor. All future meta-engine consumers (m3/m6/m9, fixpoint detector, budget tracker) route through this. Unknown-field skip is the future-proofing invariant.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Postel's law (be conservative in what you send, liberal in what you accept) applied to schema evolution
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: reader given a synthetic 3.0 row with extra field `foo:'bar'` MUST return the 2.0 fields and log a warning, not raise. reader given a 1.0_implicit row MUST return the legacy shape with deprecation warning. ANY raise on either = F-m5-i4-ReaderBrittle fires.

tier_2_backlog.blocker: performed inline in THIS witness (current_state_pre_migration §reader-inventory). Snapshot recorded. Re-run scheduled at next migration. Not a separate ship axis — folded into i6 audit emission.

strictly required only if a future need arises to roll back to 1.0 schema while preserving newly-written 2.0 rows (lossy projection). For THIS migration, .bak provides full file-replacement rollback at zero LoC cost. Down-migration script deferred until first 2.0-only data accumulates (>=R42 with no .bak).

tier_3_defer_or_split.note: git-lock guard is a 2-LoC hardening; can fold into i6 atomicity script as an early-exit check at migration runtime. Recorded for traceability, no separate axis ship.

limit_axes_documented.label: Reader discovery is open-set
limit_axes_documented.theorem: halting-problem-adjacent: cannot prove a file has no reader without exhaustive program enumeration
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: i3 inventory is a lower bound, never a complete set
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: i4 canonical accessor + code-review hygiene; treat any discovered post-hoc reader as a separate audit failure

limit_axes_documented.label: Git-lock during migration
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: migration could race a concurrent git operation
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: migrator checks for .git/index.lock pre-run; aborts with logger.error if present

limit_axes_documented.label: JSONL append vs in-place mutation tension
limit_axes_documented.theorem: JSONL convention is append-only; in-place row mutation violates the convention
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: any tool assuming row-immutability (e.g., line-offset caches, content-hash indexes) breaks
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: this migration is a documented POINT-IN-TIME exception; future schema changes prefer additive new-row emission over in-place mutation

limit_axes_documented.label: Downstream parser strictness varies
limit_axes_documented.theorem: no universal JSON-handling discipline across stdlib / pydantic / jq / awk
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: i4 canonical contract cannot bind ad-hoc parsers
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: social: code review enforces canonical accessor; technical: deprecation warning on 1.0_implicit reads

limit_axes_documented.label: Schema versioning is convention not theorem
limit_axes_documented.theorem: no JSON-spec or filesystem guarantee that schema_version is read or respected
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: the version field is honored only by cooperative readers
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: i4 mechanizes the convention for the in-house consumer set; CI/pre-commit JSON Schema validator deferred as separate axis

omega_stop_witness.method: Track-A 6 axes (i1..i6) + Track-B 5 axes (i7..i11). Each Tier-1 carries (loc_estimate, theory_anchor, falsifier) — falsifiers are concrete (sha256 .bak match, atomic-hash trichotomy abort, schema_version-presence-per-row, forward-compat unknown-field non-raise). Limit axes (i7..i11) directly ANCHOR the Tier-1 falsifiers — i7 ↔ i3 (open-set acknowledgment), i8 ↔ i6 (filesystem vs VCS), i9 ↔ i6 (in-place exception), i10 ↔ i4 (third-party drift), i11 ↔ i2 (versioning convention). Track-B is not decoration; each limit axis BINDS a Track-A control.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT(weak-with-falsifier-binding) — Tier-1 4 / Tier-2 2 / Tier-3 1 / REJECT 0 / Limit-axes 5. Sum loc_estimate(Tier-1) = 4+10+2+15 = 31 LoC (parent target was 18 for i4 alone — the witness expands the ship surface to i4-core + i2-version-bump + i6-atomicity + i4-forward-compat-reader, net 31 LoC for a complete safe-migration toolkit). The original 18 LoC parent estimate covered ONLY the migrator script body; this deepening reveals that a safe migration costs +13 LoC for atomicity guard + canonical reader, which is the expected delta for production-quality control surface.
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: second-pass enumeration over the schema-mutation surface yields 0 new axes beyond i1..i11. The mutation surface is bounded by (backup × version × discovery × reader × atomicity) × (control × limit) — a 5×2 = 10 cell matrix; i1..i11 cover 10 cells (the 11th = i9 invariant-tension folds into the atomicity column at the limit row). Self-stratification: this ω-cycle inherits Γ_0 from m5 and adds +1 for the deepening — Γ_0+1 stamp.

reader_inventory_snapshot.implication: migration is currently SAFE from breaking any executable reader; the first 2.0 reader is the i4 forward-compat accessor authored as part of this migration suite.

first_impl_target.name: i1 .bak + i6 atomicity-rename + i2 schema_version-bump as a single shippable migration toolkit (21 LoC), with i4 forward-compat reader as a paired-but-separate ship (15 LoC)
first_impl_target.rationale: i1+i6+i2 is the migration script itself — atomic, reversible (via .bak), version-tagged. i4 reader is the canonical accessor that future consumers route through. Both ships are needed before any 2.0-aware tool (m3 cert, m9 rollback, fixpoint detector i5) is written.
first_impl_target.next_after: after migration toolkit + reader land: (parent i6) historical-23-round migration writes ordinal_label values per round → (parent i5) fixpoint detector reads via i4 reader → (downstream) m3 cert populator, m9 atomic-rollback, budget tracker.
test_corpus_required_before_ship: synthetic 1.0_implicit row (no schema_version, legacy fields only) — i4 reader MUST return legacy shape with deprecation warning
synthetic 2.0 row with all 4 new fields + schema_version:'2.0' — i4 reader MUST return full 2.0 shape
synthetic 3.0 row with extra field `unknown_future_field:'value'` — i4 reader MUST skip unknown field, log warning, return 2.0 fields
atomicity test: invoke migration with deliberately corrupted tmp file — final file MUST equal pre-migration sha256 (validation rejects, atomic rename never fires)
round-trip test (when i5 down-migration ships): down_migrate(up_migrate(file_v1)) byte-equals file_v1

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_47_strategy_exploration: this trawl narrows parent's i4 axis (one tier-1 of m5 ordinal-infra) to its risk-and-control plane — meta-meta-meta-meta (parent ω-cycle = m5 implementation; THIS = i4 mutation safety surface).
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_70_multi_axis_verify: controls ↔ limits orthogonal tracks, anchored by 6+5 axes, with 4 cluster joints binding limit-anchors to control-falsifiers.
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: every Tier-1 carries a CONCRETE falsifier (sha256 .bak match, atomic hash trichotomy, schema_version per-row presence, forward-compat unknown-field non-raise). Falsifiers are syntactic per m14 Rice avoidance — no semantic 'is this safe' check; only sha256/key-presence/non-raise checks.
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: i1 mandates .bak before mutation; i6 audit emission appends to state/meta_migration_audit.jsonl with sha256_pre + sha256_post + row_count + version delta; limit_axes_documented section is permanent theorem-citation audit trail.
raw_strategies_self_applied.self_reference_acknowledgment: this ω-cycle is itself stamped Γ_0+1 — one ordinal step below parent's Γ_0 (m5 framework anchor). The witness cannot certify its own consistency (m12 Gödel-2nd, inherited transitively from parent). Mitigation: parent ω-cycle's Γ_0 is the external trust anchor; THIS witness is the deepening of one tier-1 axis and explicitly stratified one successor step below.

cross_cycle_coupling.with: parent m5 ordinal-infra ω-cycle (2026-04-26) i4 axis
cross_cycle_coupling.shared_axis: this entire trawl is the deepening of parent.tier_1[i4] (schema_migration_retroactive, 18 LoC) into a saturated 4-tier-1 / 5-limit-axis risk-and-control plane (31 LoC for migration toolkit + reader)
cross_cycle_coupling.downstream_unblock: parent.tier_1[i6] historical-23-round-migration (depends on i4 schema being live), parent.tier_1[i5] fixpoint detector (reads via i4 forward-compat reader), m3 cert populator, m9 atomic-rollback (i6 atomicity pattern is reusable), m6 falsifier substrate (F-m5-i4-* falsifiers populate it).
cross_cycle_coupling.joint_omega_stop: after this migration toolkit ships, parent.tier_1[i6] becomes implementable (back-stamps R4..R41 with linear ω+k pass-1). parent's first_impl_target chain (i1+i2 parser+comparator → i4 schema → i6 historical → i5 detector) is preserved with i4 now decomposed to (i1+i6+i2+i4-reader).
cross_cycle_coupling.shared_limits: i7 ↔ parent.i10 (m18 ordinal exhaustion analog: cannot prove reader-set boundedness), i8 ↔ raw 77 audit-append-only (filesystem atomicity is the substrate), i11 ↔ parent.i14 (retroactive integrity: schema_version is the meta-equivalent of axes_acted event-match — both are conventions enforcing integrity, neither is theorem-grade)
